name: Foundations 0: WordPress
description: Get started with WordPress using WordPress Playground. Learn what WordPress is, why its freedoms matter, how it has changed over time, and what you found by exploring WordPress Playground, based on your skill level.
learningObjectives: Can launch a WordPress Playground (https://playground.wordpress.net/) instance, navigate it and publish a first post.
Can explain the four freedoms of WordPress and why they matter
Can describe key moments in the history of WordPress
Can recognize WordPress Playground as a space for learning, testing, and experimentation
Can explain one surprising or delightful thing discovered in WordPress Playground

name: Foundations 1: Identity
description: Create a polished written statement of purpose that clearly communicates your professional motivation, direction, and value.
learningObjectives: Can clarify the professional interests, values, and strengths that shape your sense of purpose
Can recognize how audience and context affect the way purpose is communicated
Can explain your professional motivation and direction in a clear written form
Can improve a written statement for clarity, tone, and accessibility
Can produce a polished written statement of purpose

name: Foundations 2: Technology
description: Create a clear written statement that identifies your technology stack, what you still need to learn, and how you plan to learn it.
learningObjectives: Can identify the main tools, platforms, and systems relevant to your target role or field
Can distinguish between the technologies you already know and those you still need to learn
Can recognize different ways of learning new technologies effectively
Can organize your current and future technology needs into a coherent personal technology stack
Can produce a clear written statement describing your technology stack and learning plan

name: Foundations 3: Information
description: Create a short explanation of why organization strategies matter and how your target field approaches organizing information, tasks, or resources.
learningObjectives: Can explain why organization strategies matter in work and learning
Can recognize common problems caused by weak organization
Can identify ways your target field organizes information, tasks, or resources
Can compare personal habits with organizational approaches used in your field
Can produce a short explanation of why organization matters and how your field approaches it

name: Foundations 4: Self-Management
description: Create a simple personal focus plan that names what helps you start, stay on task, and recover when distracted.
learningObjectives: Can recognize personal patterns that affect focus and follow-through
Can identify common distractions and barriers to getting started
Can identify strategies that support task initiation and sustained attention
Can recognize ways to recover focus after interruption or distraction
Can produce a simple personal focus plan

name: Foundations 5: Social Impact
description: Create a short social impact statement explaining who you want your work to help, what kind of change you want to contribute to, and why that matters to you.
learningObjectives: Can identify a community, group, or issue you want your work to support
Can explain a problem or challenge affecting that community, group, or issue
Can describe the kind of positive change you want to contribute to
Can connect your desired impact to your personal values and goals
Can produce a short social impact statement

name: Foundations 6: Influence
description: Create a curated influence map that identifies the key tastemakers, organizations, and individuals shaping your target field and explains how they connect to your purpose, technology path, organizational approach, self-management habits, and social impact goals.
learningObjectives: Can identify organizations that shape standards, opportunities, or direction in your target field
Can identify individuals whose ideas, work, or reputation influence that field
Can recognize the platforms, communities, or channels where ideas in the field are shaped and shared
Can explain how influential people and organizations align with your own goals, learning needs, work habits, and desired impact
Can synthesize key field influencers into a coherent influence map connected to your broader professional foundation
